name: Potstickers
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup spelt flour or whole wheat flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the vital wheat gluten flour, spelt flour (or whole wheat), and cold water until a dense dough forms. Add more flour if needed.
2. Knead for a few minutes without folding over to avoid air pockets.
3. Cut the dough into 10 chunks and let rest for a few minutes.
4. Boil about 1 hour in a large pot using a broth made with water, white wine, soy sauce, nutritional yeast, garlic, and ginger. The gluten will puff up so leave space at the top of the pot.
5. Let cool. Properly made gluten will become dense when cooled.
6. In a food processor, chop 5 large carrots, rehydrated dried shiitake mushrooms, scallions cut into 2-inch pieces, salt, rice wine, cornstarch, white pepper.
7. Add the chopped ingredients to a bowl and mix in finely shredded cabbage (or processed if needed).
8. Press liquid from half of the gluten dough and process until similar texture to cooked ground meat. Add to filling mixture.
9. Mix 3 cups unbleached flour with boiling water to form a soft dough.
10. Knead for 5 minutes, adding extra flour as needed to avoid sticking.
11. Divide into small balls, roll out to about 4-inch diameter, place filling in the center, pinch several pleats on one side and fold over.
12. Place finished potstickers on a floured plate.
13. Steam until skins become translucent using layers of cabbage leaves in a bamboo steamer.
14. Mix dipping sauce with soy sauce, rice vinegar, chopped scallion, grated fresh ginger, and sugar (if desired).
